questions.text: Black holes have a very strong gravitational field from which nothing can escape, including light. Approximately, what is the radius of a black hole having mass equal to the sun? Hint: Use Newtonian mechanics.

choices.E: The mass is too small to be a black hole

questions.text: Surface tension is defined as the force per unit length acting on the surface of a liquid. Due to
Consider the setup below. Let $a_1, a_2, a_3$ be the accelerations of $m_1, m_2, m_3$ respectively. Which of the following is true?
questions.diagram_description: Pulley system with mass m1 on top and masses m2 and m3 hanging from pulleys below

questions.text: A ring of radius r (and moment of inertia with respect to an axis going through its center of mass $I = mr^2$) is given an initial velocity $v_0$. The ring slides for a little while and then starts to roll. What's the velocity of the ring when it rolls without slipping?

The speed of a falling raindrop is 10 m/s. The weather forecast predicts 10mm of rain in the next hour. If you go out with an umbrella of radius 70 cm, approximately what is the force of the rain hitting your umbrella?
A kitchen towel hangs off a table. The coefficient of friction between towel and table is $\mu$. What is the maximum fraction of the towel that can hang before the towel falls?
Neglecting air resistance, a baseball player on Earth can throw a baseball a distance of 100 m. If gravity is 5 times weaker on the moon, how far could he throw the ball if he were on the moon?

You live on the 20th floor of a building with two elevators, A and B, of the same mass. To go to floor 20, elevator A accelerates for the first 10 floors, then decelerates for the last 10. Meanwhile elevator B accelerates for 5 floors, moves at a constant speed for 10 floors, then decelerates for 5 floors. Both elevators complete the trip in the same amount of time. You are environmentally conscious, so you want to take the elevator which uses the least electricity. Assume friction in the motors and air drag are negligible. Which elevator should you take?

choices.C: Both elevators use the same amount of electricity.

questions.text: A mass hangs from the ceiling, held up by two springs in series. The upper spring has spring constant $k_1$, and the bottom spring has spring constant $k_2$. If the mass is displaced from equilibrium by a small amount $x$, it has a maximum speed $v$. If instead the mass is displaced by $x/2$, what is the maximum speed?
A car is speeding up as it goes in a circle. When it is at the northernmost point of the circle, what are the possible directions for its acceleration.
A regular n-gon is a polygon with n equal edges as well as equal angles. Positive charges each of
In the circuit below each resistor has equal resistance. Where should a battery be connected so that each resistor has voltage equal to the emf of the battery?
questions.diagram_description: Circuit diagram showing four points A, B, C, D with resistors connected between them in what appears to be a bridge configuration

questions.text: An electron with an initial velocity v enters a uniform field making an angle α with the magnetic field B. It then hits a screen located at a distance D form the entrance point. Assuming 0 < α < 90°, which of the following statement is true?

choices.a: The time to hit the screen increases if B increases.
choices.b: The time to hit the screen increases if B decreases.
choices.c: The time to hit the screen increases if α increases.
choices.d: The time to hit the screen increases if α decreases.
choices.e: The time to hit the screen is independent of α.

questions.text: In the following circuit, each resistor has resistance 1 Ω. Find the equivalent resistance of the circuit between point A and B.
questions.diagram_description: Complex resistor network diagram showing multiple resistors arranged in a symmetric pattern between points A and B

questions.text: Wires A and B lie in the $x - y$ plane. The wires carry equal currents in the same direction. A charged particle passes precisely between the two wires, heading in the $+z$ direction. What happens to the velocity $\vec{v}$ of the particle over time?

choices.b: The direction changes but the magnitude remains constant.
choices.c: The direction remains constant but the magnitude changes.
choices.d: The direction and magnitude both remain constant.

questions.text: A permanent magnet is dropped north-first through a conducting loop. Which diagram could represent the current in the loop as a function of time? (A positive value indicates a clockwise current, and a negative value a counterclockwise current, when viewed from above.)
questions.diagram_description: Four graphs labeled A, B, C, and D showing current vs time. Graph A shows a positive peak. Graph B shows a negative dip followed by return to baseline. Graph C shows a positive peak followed by a negative dip. Graph D shows a positive peak followed by a deeper negative dip.

questions.text: Two charges A and B are held at a fixed distance apart. A material M is placed between the charges, where M is either a block of aluminum or a block of rubber. M does not touch the charges. What happens to the magnitude of the electromagnetic force on charge A as a result of the presence of the material?

choices.c: Remains approximately the same for both materials.
choices.d: Increases for one material, decreases for the other.
choices.e: Increases for one material, remains same for the other.
choices.f: Decreases for one material, remains same for the other.

questions.text: Two permanent magnets A and B are held at a fixed distance apart. A material M is placed between the magnets, where M is either a block of aluminum or a block of rubber. M does not touch the magnets. What happens to the magnitude of the electromagnetic force on magnet A as a result of the presence of the material?

In the circuit below, all resistors are identical with resistance R and all capacitors are identical with capacitance C. A battery with voltage V is connected between points A and D. Initially, the switch is opened and all of the capacitors are uncharged. Which of the following options is true right after the switch is closed? Assume the internal resistance of the battery is negligible.

choices.a: Most of the current only flows through all resistors.
choices.b: Most of the current only flows through resistors AB, FE, and CD.
choices.c: Most of the current only flows through resistors OB, OF, and OD.
choices.d: Almost no current flows through any resistor.

questions.diagram_description: Circuit diagram showing a network of resistors and capacitors connected between points A and D, with resistors and capacitors arranged in a symmetric pattern with point O at the center

questions.text: In the circuit below, all resistors are identical with resistance R and all capacitors are identical with capacitance C. A battery with voltage V is connected between points A and D. Initially, the switch is opened and all of the capacitors are uncharged. Which of the following options is true a long time after the switch is opened? Assume the internal resistance of the battery is negligible.

choices.a: Most of the current only flows through all capacitors.
choices.b: Most of the current only flows through resistors OF, FE, and OD.
choices.c: Most of the current only flows through resistors AO, OB, and OD.
choices.d: Almost no current flows through any capacitors.

questions.diagram_description: Circuit diagram showing a network of resistors and capacitors connected between points A and D, similar to question 19 but with slight variations in the arrangement

questions.text: One mole of an ideal gas inside an engine goes through a process labelled by 1 → 2 → 3 → 1. The Volume vs Temperature graph during this process is shown below.
questions.diagram_description: Volume vs Temperature graph showing a thermodynamic cycle with three stages labeled 1, 2, and 3

questions.text: How does increasing the pressure affect the freezing temperature $T_f$ and boiling temperature $T_b$ of most liquids?

The gas in the Earth's atmosphere is more dense closer to the Earth's surface. Based on this fact, where do you expect the sun to be when you look directly at it? (Assume the sun isn't directly above you.)

choices.c: Slightly right of where it appears
choices.d: Slightly left of where it appears

questions.text: You want to magnify an ant using a magnifying glass with a focal length of 20 cm. How far should you place your magnifying glass from the ant?
Two very large mirrors intersect each other at the right angle. Suppose, you are standing at an equal distance from each mirror. How many images of yourself will you see in the mirrors?
Write down the conditions for the phase difference, φ, that causes the transmitted light to have: i. Maximum intensity ii. Minimum intensity
questions.diagram_description: Two diagrams showing polarizer setups. First diagram shows unpolarized incident light hitting a single polarizer with polarization axis at angle θ. Second diagram shows a birefringent crystal placed between two linear polarizers - the first polarizer aligned θ = 45° clockwise with respect to the y-axis, and the second polarizer aligned θ = 45° counter-clockwise with respect to the y-axis. The crystal has thickness d and refractive indices n_o and n_e.

questions.text: Find the minimum thickness of the crystal, so that the transmitted light: i. Appears blue. ii. Appears yellow.
